column27: 1.13 FATIGUE  Over the past week, have you usually felt fatigued? This feeling is not part of being sleepy or sad.    Au cours de la semaine précédente, vous êtes vous senti(e) habituellement fatigué(e) ? Ce sentiment  ne fait pas partie du fait d'avoir s
column28: 2.1 SPEECH  Over the past week, have you had problems with your speech?
column29: 2.2 SALIVA & DROOLING  Over the past week, have you usually had too much saliva during when you are awake or when you sleep?
column30: 2.3 CHEWING AND SWALLOWING  Over the past week, have you usually had problems swallowing pills or eating meals? Do you need your pills cut or crushed or your meals to be made soft, chopped or blended to avoid choking?
column31: 2.4 EATING TASKS  Over the past week, have you usually had troubles handling your food and using eating utensils? For example, do you have trouble handling finger foods or using forks, knifes, spoons, chopsticks?
column32: 2.5 DRESSING  Over the past week, have you usually had problems dressing? For example, are you slow or do you need help with buttoning, using zippers, putting on or taking off your clothes or jewelry?
column33: 2.6 HYGIENE  Over the past week, have you usually been slow or do you need help with washing, bathing, shaving, brushing teeth, combing your hair or with other personal hygiene?
column34: 2.7 HANDWRITING  Over the past week, have people usually had trouble reading your handwriting?
column35: 2.8 DOING HOBBIES AND OTHER ACTIVITIES  Over the past week, have you usually had trouble doing your hobbies or other things that you like to do?
column36: 2.9 TURNING IN BED  Over the past week, do you usually have trouble turning over in bed?
column37: 2.10 TREMOR  Over the past week, have you usually had shaking or tremor?
column38: 2.11 GETTING OUT OF BED, A CAR, OR A DEEP CHAIR  Over the past week, have you usually had trouble getting out of bed, a car seat, or a deep chair?
column39: 2.12 WALKING AND BALANCE  Over the past week, have you usually had problems with balance and walking?
column40: 2.13 FREEZING  Over the past week, on your usual day when walking, do you suddenly stop or freeze as if your feet are stuck to the floor.

column8: 3: Moderate: Cognitive deficits interfere with but do not preclude the patient's ability to carry out normal activities and social interactions.
column10: 0: Normal: No hallucinations or psychotic behaviour.
column12: 1: Slight: Episodes of depressed mood that are not sustained for more than one day at a time. No interference with patient's ability to carry out normal activities and social interactions.
column14: 3: Moderate: Anxious feelings interfere with, but do not preclude, the patient's ability to carry out normal activities and social interactions.
column16: 3: Moderate: Apathy interferes with most activities and social interactions.
column21: 3: Moderate/Modéré: Sleep problems cause a lot of difficulties getting a full night of sleep, but I still usually sleep for more than half the night./Des problèmes de sommeil entrainent beaucoup de difficultés à avoir une nuit complète de sommeil, mais je dors encore habituellement plus de la moitié de la nuit.
column22: 2: Mild/Léger: Sometimes I fall asleep when alone and relaxing. For example, while reading or watching TV./Une somnolence diurne apparaît mais je peux résister et rester éveillé(e).
column23: 3: Moderate/Modéré: These feelings cause a lot of problems, but they do not stop me from doing things or being with other people./Ces sensations me procurent beaucoup de problèmes, mais ne m'empêchent pas d'effectuer des choses ou d'être avec d'autres personnes.
column24: 2: Mild/Léger: Urine problems cause some difficulties with my daily activities. However, I do not have urine accidents./Les problèmes urinaires causent quelques difficultés dans mes activités quotidiennes. Cependant, je n'ai pas d'accidents urinaires.
column26: 2: Mild/Léger: Dizzy or foggy feelings cause me to hold on to something, but I do not need to sit or lie back down./Des sensations de vertige ou de brouillard m'obligent à me tenir à quelque chose, mais je n'ai pas besoin de m'asseoir ou de me recoucher.
column27: 3: Moderate/Modéré: Fatigue causes me a lot of troubles doing things or being with people. However, it does not stop me from doing anything./La fatigue me cause beaucoup de problèmes à faire les choses ou être avec les gens. Cependant, elle ne m'empêche pas de faire quoi que ce soit.
column28: 2: Mild: My speech causes people to ask me to occasionally repeat myself, but not everyday.
